fields.title: Review Lecture Notes
fields.description: Spend 15 minutes reviewing notes from the most recent lecture for one of your classes. Identify any confusing points and jot them down to ask your professor or a classmate.

fields.title: Organize Digital Files
fields.description: Dedicate 20 minutes to organizing files on your laptop or cloud storage. Create new folders, rename files for clarity, and delete unnecessary documents to improve efficiency.

fields.title: Plan Tomorrow's Schedule
fields.description: Take 10 minutes at the end of the day to outline your schedule for the next day. List your top priorities, appointments, and study blocks to ensure a productive day.

fields.title: Quick Exercise Break
fields.description: Engage in a 15-minute physical activity. This could be a brisk walk, some stretching, a short yoga routine, or a quick bodyweight workout to boost energy and focus.

fields.title: Respond to Unread Emails
fields.description: Allocate 20 minutes to clear your email inbox. Respond to important messages, archive or delete less critical ones, and unsubscribe from unwanted newsletters.

fields.title: Binary Search Implementation
fields.description: Implement binary search iteratively AND recursively. Test it on a sorted list of 20 integers, searching for both present and absent values. Submission Requirement: Upload your .py or .js file with both implementations and test output.

fields.title: JWT Auth Middleware
fields.description: Write a middleware function for Express.js or Django that validates a JWT token from the Authorization header and returns 401 if invalid or missing. Submission Requirement: Upload your middleware file with comments explaining each step.

fields.title: Index a Database Table
fields.description: Given a users table with 1 million rows, write the SQL to add an index on email, explain why it helps, and write a query that uses it effectively. Submission Requirement: Upload a .sql or .txt file with your CREATE INDEX statement, sample query, and explanation.

fields.title: Global State with Context API
fields.description: Build a React app with a ThemeContext that toggles between dark and light mode. At least 3 components must consume the context without prop drilling. Submission Requirement: Upload your Context file and one consumer component.

fields.title: Docker Compose Setup
fields.description: Write a docker-compose.yml that spins up a web app (any framework) and a PostgreSQL database with environment variables, volume mounts, and a health check. Submission Requirement: Upload your docker-compose.yml file.

fields.title: Binary Search Implementation
fields.description: Implement binary search iteratively AND recursively. Test it on a sorted list of 20 integers, searching for both present and absent values. Submission Requirement: Upload your .py or .js file with both implementations and test output.

fields.title: JWT Auth Middleware
fields.description: Write a middleware function for Express.js or Django that validates a JWT token from the Authorization header and returns 401 if invalid or missing. Submission Requirement: Upload your middleware file with comments explaining each step.

fields.title: Index a Database Table
fields.description: Given a users table with 1 million rows, write the SQL to add an index on email, explain why it helps, and write a query that uses it effectively. Submission Requirement: Upload a .sql or .txt file with your CREATE INDEX statement, sample query, and explanation.

fields.title: Global State with Context API
fields.description: Build a React app with a ThemeContext that toggles between dark and light mode. At least 3 components must consume the context without prop drilling. Submission Requirement: Upload your Context file and one consumer component.

fields.title: Docker Compose Setup
fields.description: Write a docker-compose.yml that spins up a web app (any framework) and a PostgreSQL database with environment variables, volume mounts, and a health check. Submission Requirement: Upload your docker-compose.yml file.

fields.title: Reverse a Linked List
fields.description: Implement a function that reverses a singly linked list in-place using Python or JavaScript. Your solution must handle edge cases: empty list and single node. Submission Requirement: Upload your .py or .js file with the function and at least 3 test cases printed to console.

fields.title: Build a REST API Endpoint
fields.description: Create a GET /products endpoint using Django REST Framework or Express.js that returns a paginated list of 10 items per page, supports ?search= query param, and returns proper HTTP status codes. Submission Requirement: Upload your views/routes file.

fields.title: SQL Query Optimization
fields.description: Given a slow query: SELECT * FROM orders JOIN users ON orders.user_id = users.id WHERE users.city = 'Delhi', rewrite it using indexed columns, avoid SELECT *, and add a LIMIT. Submission Requirement: Upload a .txt or .sql file with your optimized query and a brief explanation.

fields.title: React Counter with useReducer
fields.description: Build a React counter component using useReducer (not useState) that supports increment, decrement, reset, and step-size input. Submission Requirement: Upload your .jsx component file.

fields.title: Git Branching Workflow
fields.description: Create a local git repo, make a main branch, create a feature/login branch, commit 3 changes, then merge it back to main resolving a deliberate conflict. Submission Requirement: Upload a screenshot of git log --oneline --graph output as a .png or .txt.

fields.title: Linked List
fields.description: Reverse a list.

fields.title: Implement a Singly Linked List
fields.description: Implement a `SinglyLinkedList` class with methods for `append`, `prepend`, `delete_node` (by value), `find` (by value), and `print_list`. Each node should store an integer value.

fields.title: Anagram Checker
fields.description: Write a function `are_anagrams(str1, str2)` that takes two strings as input and returns `True` if they are anagrams of each other, `False` otherwise. Consider edge cases like empty strings, strings with spaces, and case sensitivity.

fields.title: Basic Library System Classes
fields.description: Design and implement classes for a simplified library system. You should have a `Book` class (with title, author, ISBN) and a `Library` class (which can add books, list available books, and check out/return books). Focus on class design and basic functionality.

fields.title: CSV Data Aggregator
fields.description: Write a script that reads a CSV file (e.g., `data.csv` with columns: `Name`, `Category`, `Value`), calculates the sum of `Value` for each unique `Category`, and prints the results. You can create a sample `data.csv` for testing.

fields.title: Generate Nth Fibonacci Number (Recursive & Iterative)
fields.description: Write a function `get_fibonacci_number(n)` that returns the Nth Fibonacci number. Implement it using both an iterative approach and a recursive approach. Compare their efficiency. Handle cases where `n` is 0 or 1.

fields.title: Reverse a Linked List
fields.description: Implement a function that reverses a singly linked list in-place. Submission: Upload .py or .js file.

fields.title: Build a REST API
fields.description: Create a GET /products endpoint. Submission: Upload views file.

fields.title: SQL Optimization
fields.description: Rewrite a slow JOIN query. Submission: Upload .sql file.

fields.title: React useReducer
fields.description: Build a counter component using useReducer. Submission: Upload .jsx file.

fields.title: Git Workflow
fields.description: Resolve a merge conflict. Submission: Upload git log.

fields.title: Implement a Singly Linked List
fields.description: Implement a `SinglyLinkedList` class in your preferred language. It should support `add(value)`, `remove(value)`, `contains(value)`, and `size()` methods. Ensure proper handling of edge cases (empty list, removing non-existent elements).

fields.title: CSV Product Inventory Processor
fields.description: Write a program that reads product data from a CSV file (e.g., `product_id,name,price,quantity`). It should calculate the total value of the inventory (sum of `price * quantity` for all products) and identify the product with the highest quantity. Output both results to the console. You can assume a simple CSV format without escaped commas.

fields.title: Basic Library Catalog System
fields.description: Design and implement classes for a very basic library catalog system. You should have at least two classes: `Book` (with properties like `title`, `author`, `ISBN`) and `Library` (which manages a collection of `Book` objects and provides methods like `add_book`, `find_book_by_isbn`, `list_all_books`, and `remove_book`).

fields.title: Palindrome Checker from File
fields.description: Create a program that reads a list of words, one per line, from a text file. For each word, it should determine if it's a palindrome (reads the same forwards and backwards, ignoring case and non-alphanumeric characters like spaces or punctuation). Output each word along with `(Palindrome)` or `(Not Palindrome)` to a new output file.

fields.title: Recursive Fibonacci Sequence Generator
fields.description: Write a function `fibonacci(n)` that returns the `n`-th number in the Fibonacci sequence using recursion. The sequence starts 0, 1, 1, 2, 3... (i.e., `fibonacci(0)` should return 0, `fibonacci(1)` should return 1). Include basic error handling for negative inputs and consider memoization as an optional challenge.

fields.title: Anagram Checker
fields.description: Write a function that takes two strings as input and determines if they are anagrams of each other. The function should ignore case and non-alphabetic characters (e.g., 'A decimal point' and 'I'm a dot in place' should be considered anagrams).

fields.title: Simple Library Management System
fields.description: Design and implement classes for `Book` and `Library`. The `Book` class should have attributes like `title`, `author`, `ISBN`, and `is_available`. The `Library` class should manage a collection of `Book` objects, allowing operations such as `add_book`, `remove_book`, `borrow_book`, `return_book`, and `list_available_books`.

fields.title: CSV Data Processor
fields.description: Write a script that reads a CSV file (assume it exists, e.g., `data.csv` with columns `name,age,city`). The script should parse the data, calculate the average age of all entries, and then write a new CSV file (`summary.csv`) containing only entries where the age is above the calculated average. The new file should only include the `name` and `city` columns.

fields.title: Prime Number Sieve
fields.description: Implement the Sieve of Eratosthenes algorithm to find all prime numbers up to a given limit `N`. Your function should take `N` as an argument and return a list of all primes less than or equal to `N`.

fields.title: Command-Line Contact Manager
fields.description: Create a command-line application that allows users to perform basic CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations on a list of contacts. Each contact should have a `name`, `phone_number`, and `email`. Store contacts in an in-memory data structure (e.g., a list of dictionaries or objects). Commands could include: `add <name> <phone> <email>`, `view_all`, `search <name_or_email_partial>`, `update <old_name> <new_name> <new_phone> <new_email>`, and `delete <name>`.

fields.title: Implement a Singly Linked List
fields.description: Create a class `SinglyLinkedList` with methods for `append`, `prepend`, `delete` (by value), `find` (by value), and `printList`. Each node should have `value` and `next` attributes. Include basic error handling (e.g., deleting a non-existent element).

fields.title: Palindrome Checker and Longest Palindromic Substring
fields.description: Write two functions: 1) `isPalindrome(s)` that returns true if a string `s` is a palindrome (case-insensitive, ignores non-alphanumeric characters). 2) `longestPalindrome(s)` that finds and returns the longest palindromic substring within `s`.

fields.title: Design a Simple Library Management System
fields.description: Design and implement classes like `Book`, `Member`, and `Library`. The `Library` class should manage a collection of books and members, allowing for adding/removing books, registering/deregistering members, borrowing books, and returning books. Consider attributes like `title`, `author`, `ISBN` for `Book`, and `name`, `member_id`, `borrowed_books` for `Member`. The system should prevent borrowing an unavailable book or returning a book not borrowed.

fields.title: Binary Tree Traversals and Depth
fields.description: Implement a `TreeNode` class with `value`, `left`, and `right` attributes. Create functions to perform in-order, pre-order, and post-order traversals on a given binary tree, returning lists of node values. Additionally, implement a function `maxDepth(root)` that calculates the maximum depth (or height) of the tree.

fields.title: CSV Data Analyzer
fields.description: Write a program that reads a CSV file (you can create a sample one with columns like `Name`, `Age`, `City`, `Score`). The program should: 1) Calculate and print the average score. 2) Find and print the name of the person with the highest score. 3) Prompt the user for a city name and list all people from that city. Handle potential `FileNotFoundError`.

fields.title: Reverse a Linked List
fields.description: Implement a function that reverses a singly linked list in-place. Submission: Upload .py or .js file.

fields.title: Build a REST API
fields.description: Create a GET /products endpoint. Submission: Upload views file.

fields.title: SQL Optimization
fields.description: Rewrite a slow JOIN query. Submission: Upload .sql file.

fields.title: React useReducer
fields.description: Build a counter component using useReducer. Submission: Upload .jsx file.

fields.title: Git Workflow
fields.description: Resolve a merge conflict. Submission: Upload git log.

fields.title: Implement a Generic Stack Data Structure
fields.description: Implement a generic Stack data structure in your preferred language. The stack should support `push`, `pop`, `peek`, `is_empty`, and `size` operations. Ensure proper error handling for popping from an empty stack.

fields.title: Object-Oriented Bookstore Management
fields.description: Design and implement an object-oriented system for managing a small bookstore. Create `Book` and `Author` classes, and a `Bookstore` class. The `Book` class should have attributes like title, author(s), ISBN, price, and quantity. The `Bookstore` class should manage a collection of `Book` objects, allowing operations such as `add_book`, `remove_book`, `search_by_title_or_author`, `update_book_quantity`, and `list_all_books`.

fields.title: Log File Analyzer
fields.description: Write a script that reads a text-based log file (you can create a dummy one with lines like 'ERROR: Failed to connect', 'INFO: User logged in', 'WARNING: Low disk space'). The script should parse the log file, count the occurrences of each log level (ERROR, INFO, WARNING), and then output these counts, along with a list of all unique error messages encountered.

fields.title: Basic Weather Data Fetcher
fields.description: Use a public weather API (e.g., OpenWeatherMap - requires an API key, or WeatherAPI.com) to fetch current weather data for a user-specified city. The script should prompt the user for a city name, make the API request, parse the JSON response, and then print relevant information like temperature, humidity, and a brief description of the weather conditions.

fields.title: Longest Common Subsequence
fields.description: Implement an algorithm to find the length of the longest common subsequence (LCS) between two given strings. For example, the LCS of 'AGGTAB' and 'GXTXAYB' is 'GTAB' with length 4. Consider both recursive and dynamic programming approaches.

fields.title: Reverse a Linked List
fields.description: Implement a function that reverses a singly linked list in-place. Submission: Upload .py or .js file.

fields.title: Build a REST API
fields.description: Create a GET /products endpoint. Submission: Upload views file.

fields.title: SQL Optimization
fields.description: Rewrite a slow JOIN query. Submission: Upload .sql file.

fields.title: React useReducer
fields.description: Build a counter component using useReducer. Submission: Upload .jsx file.

fields.title: Git Workflow
fields.description: Resolve a merge conflict. Submission: Upload git log.

fields.title: Reverse a Linked List
fields.description: Implement a function that reverses a singly linked list in-place. Submission: Upload .py or .js file.

fields.title: Build a REST API
fields.description: Create a GET /products endpoint. Submission: Upload views file.

fields.title: SQL Optimization
fields.description: Rewrite a slow JOIN query. Submission: Upload .sql file.

fields.title: React useReducer
fields.description: Build a counter component using useReducer. Submission: Upload .jsx file.

fields.title: Git Workflow
fields.description: Resolve a merge conflict. Submission: Upload git log.

fields.title: Implement a Basic Stack Data Structure
fields.description: Create a class `Stack` that supports the following operations: `push(item)`, `pop()`, `peek()`, and `isEmpty()`. The stack should store elements and follow the Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) principle. Handle cases like popping from an empty stack gracefully (e.g., by raising an exception or returning `None`).

fields.title: Build a Simple Library System (OOP)
fields.description: Design and implement a simple library system using Object-Oriented Programming principles. Create classes for `Book` (with attributes like title, author, ISBN) and `Library` (to manage a collection of books). The `Library` class should have methods to `addBook(book)`, `removeBook(isbn)`, and `findBookByTitle(title)`.

fields.title: CSV File Processor and Analyzer
fields.description:
Write a script that reads a simple CSV file (e.g., `name,age,city,score`). The script should parse the data and then be able to: 
1. Print the average score of all entries.
2. Find and print the name of the person with the highest score.
Assume the CSV has a header row and no complex quoting.

fields.title: Recursive Palindrome Checker
fields.description: Develop a function `is_palindrome(text)` that determines whether a given string is a palindrome (reads the same forwards and backward). The function should ignore case sensitivity and non-alphanumeric characters. Implement the core logic using recursion.

fields.title: Command-Line To-Do List
fields.description:
Create a basic command-line application for managing a to-do list. The program should allow users to:
1. `add <task_description>`: Add a new task.
2. `list`: Display all current tasks with their status (e.g., `[ ] Task 1`, `[x] Task 2`).
3. `complete <task_index>`: Mark a task as complete (by its index in the list).
No need for file persistence; tasks can be stored in memory for the duration of the program.
